adrs.title: Markdown project docs
adrs.author: tejas
adrs.summary: Originally we kept PM docs as plain markdown for simplicity.
adrs.context: We wanted project context in the repo and easy to diff in a PR. Markdown is the obvious default.
adrs.decision: Chose plain markdown for the first cut.
adrs.consequences: Superseded by ADR-2. Long markdown went unread by humans and was lossy for the rich, scannable views we wanted.

adrs.title: Separate data from presentation
adrs.author: tejas
adrs.summary: Structured data is the source of truth; the hub is generated, read-only HTML.
adrs.context: We need humans to skim HTML, agents to edit reliably, and git to diff cleanly — all from one source.

options.text: Data is truth; generate read-only HTML

adrs.decision: Author structured data; a generator renders read-only HTML. Never hand-edit the HTML.
adrs.consequences: Clean, reviewable diffs and reliable agent edits — at the cost of requiring a build step to regenerate the hub.

adrs.title: One offline, committed HTML file
adrs.author: tejas
adrs.summary: Generate a single self-contained index.html, commit it, open offline.
adrs.context: How should a human view the hub with the least friction — no server, no network?
adrs.decision: Generate one self-contained index.html, commit it, and open it at file:// with zero dependencies.
adrs.consequences: Works fully offline from any checkout; marked linguist-generated so diffs stay collapsed.

adrs.title: Flat-merge JSON data islands
adrs.author: opus
adrs.summary: Four focused JSON files merge into one flat #pm-data island at build time.
adrs.context: One large data file is conflict-prone; deeply nested data is awkward to edit.
adrs.decision: Author core, tasks, spec, and content; the build flat-merges them into one island.
adrs.consequences: Smaller, focused diffs and a simple runtime read; the build owns the merge step.

adrs.title: Single-writer rule for agent fleets
adrs.author: tejas
adrs.summary: Sub-agents read and return results; the parent is the sole writer to PM/ data.
adrs.context: A parent agent allocates work to sub-agents on demand; concurrent writes would corrupt the hub.

options.text: Every agent writes directly to PM/ data
Parent is sole writer; sub-agents return results

adrs.decision: Sub-agents read context and return structured results. The parent serializes all writes; only the activity log is append-only.
adrs.consequences: No concurrent-write corruption and fully reviewable fleet output; the parent becomes a clear serialization point.

adrs.title: Calm command center aesthetic
adrs.author: tejas
adrs.summary: Linear/Height energy: cool neutrals, one blue accent, semantic status colors, system fonts.
adrs.context: The hub must be instantly scannable, not flashy or noisy.
adrs.decision: Cool-neutral oklch grays, a single blue accent, semantic status colors, and system fonts only.
adrs.consequences: Fast to scan, no dependency on web fonts, and consistent light and dark themes.

adrs.title: Per-entity data files
adrs.author: opus
adrs.summary: Proposed: split tasks/ADRs/debug into one file per entity for cleaner history.
adrs.context: Large projects may hit merge conflicts on a single tasks.json.
adrs.decision: Undecided. Proposed to glob a directory of per-entity files for cleaner git blame and fewer conflicts.
adrs.consequences: Better per-entity history at the cost of many more files; the build would glob a directory instead of reading one file.

adrs.title: File-based agent relay bus
adrs.author: tejas
adrs.summary: Agents coordinate through a per-file bus in PM/comms, polled on an interval.
adrs.context: Multiple agent sessions in separate terminals can't talk; coordination meant manual copy-paste. We need messages + task handoffs without standing up a server.
adrs.decision: Each agent writes only its own files (presence, the messages it sends, its own acks) under PM/comms; polling reads the directory. Handoffs follow an offer → accept → complete lifecycle.
adrs.consequences: Conflict-free with no server and no merge races; it travels in the repo and folds into the hub. Coordination is eventually-consistent at the poll interval rather than instant.

debug.title: Hub renders blank from the design bundle
debug.summary: The exported index.html shipped with app.js stubbed out as a placeholder, so nothing rendered.
debug.symptom: Opening the bundled index.html shows only the empty shell — no views paint.

hypotheses.note: The script body is a literal placeholder, so boot() is undefined.

repro: Inspect the <script> body — it's a placeholder
debug.rootCause: The design export stubs large scripts to keep the bundle small; the real app.js was never inlined.

fix.text: build.mjs inlines the real engine/app.js plus a fresh data island into the template.

debug.guard: The build asserts app.js is over 10KB and contains boot() before it writes index.html.

debug.title: Closing script tag in content breaks the island
debug.summary: A literal closing-script sequence inside embedded JSON ended the data script early and truncated the page.
debug.symptom: When a doc contained a closing-script string, everything below it disappeared.

hypotheses.text: The HTML parser closes the island early
hypotheses.note: The JSON is parsed as raw text; a nested close-tag ends the script.

repro: Add a doc whose body contains a closing script tag
Everything after the tag is gone
debug.rootCause: Embedded JSON must escape the script-close sequence before it lands in the HTML.

fix.text: build.mjs replaces < with its unicode escape inside the JSON island.

debug.guard: A self-check round-trips a closing-script string through the bundler and asserts the data survives.

debug.title: oklch colors fall back to black on old browsers
debug.summary: Browsers older than mid-2023 don't support the oklch() color function the theme is built on.
debug.symptom: On very old browsers the entire UI renders close to black.

hypotheses.note: Verified against caniuse and a legacy-browser VM.

repro: Open the hub in a pre-2023 browser
debug.rootCause: The theme is built entirely on oklch() color tokens with no sRGB fallback.

fix.text: Document the supported-browser baseline; evaluate sRGB fallbacks if demand warrants.

debug.guard: Track real usage; revisit fallbacks only if a meaningful share of users are affected.

debug.title: Empty debug log blanks the whole hub on boot
debug.summary: A project with no debug entries (incl. a stock rocketman init) crashed at boot — the entire hub rendered blank.
debug.symptom: Open the hub for a project whose debug log is empty; nothing paints — a blank page, no views.

hypotheses.text: The data island failed to parse
A top-level read assumes debug is non-empty
hypotheses.note: app.js ran D.debug[0].id at module load; D.debug[0] is undefined when debug is [].

repro: rocketman init a fresh project (template ships debug: [])
headless console: Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading id)
debug.rootCause: app.js dereferenced D.debug[0].id at top level during boot; with an empty debug array that threw before any view rendered, aborting the whole page.

fix.text: Guarded the boot read (D.debug[0] && ...), added a Debug empty-state, and guarded REG[inv.task] and the dashboard activity read. The renderer now tolerates empty debug/activity/owner.

debug.guard: A fresh rocketman init hub is headless-booted in verification: empty debug + empty activity + unassigned owner must render with no uncaught error.

events.line: handed RM-8 off to skills over the relay

events.detail: app.js was stubbed in the bundle · [[bug-01|BUG-1]]
Launch skills wait on the CLI ship hooks
Escape the markup in the data island · [[bug-02|BUG-2]]

events.line: created the project from the locked spec
